item.text: I’ll trace the executable code paths and documented invocations, then inspect corrected ROOT key inventories only if the existing local environment can do so without any allocation. I’ll keep the audit strictly read-only and won’t delegate.

items.text: Read governing issue/reference docs and locate all FPS adoption invocations and declared artifacts
Trace endpoint analysis, P4 validation, FPS adoption, and unified 4D adoption code paths
Inspect existing corrected FPS ROOT key inventories if local ROOT tooling is already available
Cross-check invariants, classify findings, and produce a falsifiable post-run verification checklist

The one mechanism that DOES rebuild a per-universe measured target from `w_bkg` is the **vertical sweep re-quote** — `sweep_bank_5d.py` do_run (`:177` banks `{tag}_bkgw.npy`, `:232-241` rebuilds `measured_weights` per universe, `:243-246` fail-closed unless `--allow-cv-background`) → `analyze_universes_5d.py:91-100` block-sum — plus the lateral direct-driver via `collect_bkg_nd(universe_branch=)` (`unfold_nd_omnifold_unbinned.py` ~:664). **These have NOT been run on the bkgaware inputs:** no `bank_sweep_5d`/`*_bkgw.npy` exist; `uq_5d/universe_sweep/` unfolds are dated 2026-06-28/29 (pre-fix); `sweep_bank_5d.py:39` still defaults to the non-bkgaware omnifile; RUN_LOG:1439 still lists "run the #13 covariance re-quote" as deferred. **To close:** `sweep_bank_5d.py --dump --omnifile ...bkgaware.root` → `--run` all vertical universes → `analyze_universes_5d.py` (+ lateral direct-driver on bkgaware), then compare sqrt-trace vs the CV-frozen covariance. NOTE: the **PET Phase-7 retraining-response is unaffected** (it consumes only signal truth ratios, bit-identical across banks — see `PET_UQ_PRODUCTION_STATUS.md` receipts); this gap is about C_syst-final/lateral, not Phase 7. | **RESOLVED 2026-07-14** (188-universe re-quote, both legs; <0.3% null-effect; all adopted covs PSD) | `nd-unfolding/CORRECTED_UQ_PRODUCTION_STATUS.md` (2026-07-14 B5' receipts + baseline-vs-bkgaware table); `nd-unfolding/ND_OMNIFOLD_RUN_LOG.md` (2026-07-03/04, 2026-07-12 entries); `nd-unfolding/PET_UQ_PRODUCTION_STATUS.md` (2026-07-14 CRITICAL FINDING) |

    28	| # | Trap | Detail lives in |
    29	|---|---|---|
    30	| 6 | **Never bare-`hadd` a `_universes_full` omnifile** — ROOT 100 GB TTree rollover aborts mid-merge leaving a partial missing the data+bkg trees. Use `2d-unfolding/uq/hadd_universes_full.py`. | `2d-unfolding/2D_OMNIFOLD_REFERENCE.md` §Event-loop workflow |
    31	| 7 | **Never feed the event loop a combined MEFHC manifest** — it silently applies the first playlist's flux to all 12. Run per playlist, merge after. | same |
    32	| 8 | **`hadd` sums `TParameter<double>`** — POT summing across playlists is correct; a per-file nucleon count would be inflated 12×, hence the fixed tracker constant 3.2353e30. | same |
    33	| 9 | **Pre-2026-04-25 event-loop outputs** use the IsMinosMatchMuon stub (≈10% background) — regenerate before comparing to paper numbers. | same |
    34	| 10 | **eavailW completeness double-count** (2026-06-09, FIXED): OmniFold step-2 already efficiency-corrects; an extra reco-pass completeness division inflated the data ~2×. Caught by the marginal self-validation gate — keep that gate in any new covariance script. | `docs/FUTURE_DIRECTIONS.md` tombstone → `nd-unfolding/ND_OMNIFOLD_RUN_LOG.md` (2026-06-09) |
    35	| 11 | **Stale PET `ExtraEnergyClusters_*` input** (FIXED): wrong, mostly-empty branch; point-cloud chain rebuilt from `CVUniverse::GetRecoClusters()`. | `VALIDATION_LEDGER.md` (Known Audit Findings) |
    36	| 17 | **Never run PET cross-section extraction in the TensorFlow-module Python** — it has no PyROOT. The PET replica launcher switches to the activated `root_6_28` interpreter after GPU training, and the extractor self-reexecs there so already-snapshotted jobs are safe. | `nd-unfolding/pet/{sbatch_pet_bootstrap_replica.sh,extract_bootstrap_replica.py}` |
    37	
    38	## Resume-skip validates existence, not completeness (found 2026-07-17, 4D corrected uthrow)
    39	`[[ -s $OUT ]] && skip` resume guards (used across many sbatch launchers) treat any non-empty
    40	output as done. Producers that atomic-save per-unit into one file (e.g. `unified_throw_cov.py
    41	do_throws` per-throw saves) leave VALID-but-partial files when interrupted — the resume then
    42	permanently skips them. Bit us: comb4dCc 55971617 failed on 15/160 missing throws because slabs
    43	31,34-39 were partial leftovers of an interrupted multinode run; all 40 array tasks "COMPLETED".
    44	Caught only by the combine's `--expected-throws` manifest gate. Repair: partials moved to
    45	`uq_4d/corrected/uthrow_slabs_4d/partial_20260716_interrupted/`, regen 56025478 -> combine
    46	56025481 -> adopt 56025483. Fix pattern for future launchers: content-validated resume (open the
    47	file, check unit inventory) or write-to-temp + rename-on-complete. (BEN-023.)
     1	# 2D OmniFold Study — Reference Notes
     2	
     3	Workflow invariants, recurring gotchas, and correctness contracts. Read
     4	before launching jobs or trusting outputs.
     5	
     6	---
     7	
     8	## Environment setup
     9	
    10	From the repo root (`MINERvA-OmniFold/`):
    11	
    12	```bash
    13	source setup_salloc_env.sh
    14	```
    15	
    16	That script is self-locating: it loads the `root_6_28` conda env, sources
    17	`unbinned_unfolding/build/setup.sh` (built in-tree), exports
    18	`MINERVA_PREFIX=$REPO/MINERvA101/opt`, and sources
    19	`MINERvA101/opt/bin/setup.sh`. No HOME paths involved.
    20	
    21	## Archiving conventions
    22	
    23	Stale logs and one-off artifacts that we keep for reference but don't want
    24	in the active tree go to a dated archive directory at the OLD scratch root,
    25	**outside the new repo**:
    26	
    27	```
    28	/pscratch/sd/j/josephrb/MINERvA101/archive_pre_migration_<YYYY-MM-DD>/
    29	```
    30	
    31	Rules:
    32	
    33	- Active outputs (current `.root`, `.png`, recent job logs) live in
    34	  `2d-unfolding/`.
    35	- Once superseded, move job logs (`*_<jobid>.{out,err}`), throwaway dirs
    36	  (`Doc_tmp/`, scratch scripts), and orphaned PDFs into a new dated
    37	  `archive_pre_migration_*` (or `archive_<purpose>_*`) folder under the OLD
    38	  scratch root.
    39	- In-tree archives are gitignored via `2d-unfolding/archive_*/` (see
    40	  `.gitignore`). Anything still in the OLD scratch tree is outside git
    41	  entirely.
    42	- Heavy intermediates (`runEventLoopOmniFold_*.root`, `evloop_work_*/`)
    43	  are gitignored by the global `*.root` rule and `evloop_work_*/` rule;
    44	  they may be copied into `2d-unfolding/` as needed without being tracked.
    45	
    46	Canonical runtime binary: `opt/bin/runEventLoopOmniFold` (the path exported
    47	by `opt/bin/setup.sh`). Do **not** call build-tree copies — duplicate
    48	build-tree executables have silently shadowed the installed patched binary
    49	in the past.
    50	
    51	## SLURM script conventions
    52	
    53	- **Interactive-first.** Before submitting an sbatch, check
    54	  `squeue -u $USER` for a live `interactive` allocation with time left.
    55	  NERSC's regular and shared queues routinely sit on `Priority` for
    56	  minutes-to-hours; an existing interactive allocation can run the same
    57	  work immediately. Use
    58	  `srun --jobid=<INTERACTIVE_JOBID> --overlap -n 1 --cpus-per-task=<N>
    59	  bash -lc '... ; source $REPO/setup_salloc_env.sh ; <command>'`
    60	  to launch onto it. Work that fits this pattern includes: the C++
    61	  build (`cmake --build && cmake --install`, ~5 min, 8-16 CPU), short
    62	  1A event-loop smokes (~5-15 min, 8-128 CPU), individual unfold runs,
    63	  Python analysis scripts. The interactive's full 128 CPU is available
    64	  inside one node even if your `salloc` requested fewer — `srun
    65	  --cpus-per-task=N` controls the slice. If you already submitted an
    66	  sbatch and *then* realized the interactive is up, `scancel` the
    67	  sbatch — leaving both running leaks compute and risks output-file
    68	  races. Reserve sbatch for genuinely multi-node arrays, multi-hour
    69	  walls, or work that must outlive the shell.
    70	- Do **not** combine `set -u` with `conda activate root_6_28`. The conda
    71	  `deactivate-root.sh` hook references `CONDA_BACKUP_ROOTSYS` and aborts
    72	  under nounset in a fresh batch shell.
    73	- Export `PYTHONUNBUFFERED=1` so Python stdout appears in the `.out` file
    74	  during the run.
    75	- **Do not use `srun`** inside 2D OmniFold sbatch scripts. On Perlmutter,
    76	  inherited `SRUN_CPUS_PER_TASK` from a live interactive allocation breaks
    77	  nested srun. Bare `python3 ...` is the safe default. (The interactive
    78	  case is the inverse: `srun --jobid=<INTERACTIVE> --overlap` is the
    79	  correct primitive when launching new work onto an existing allocation.)
    80	- See `sbatch_validate_1A_corrected.sh` and `sbatch_unfold_2d.sh` for the
    81	  working template.
    82	
    83	## Event-loop workflow
    84	
    85	- `runEventLoopOmniFold.cpp` picks **one** global playlist flux/calibration
    86	  from the first event's run number. **Never** feed it a combined MEFHC
    87	  manifest — the tool silently applies the first playlist's flux to all
    88	  events, corrupting 11/12 of the dataset.
    89	  → Run per-playlist, then `hadd` outputs.
    90	- `runEventLoopOmniFold.cpp` intentionally does **not** write
    91	  `pTmu_fiducial_nucleons` because `hadd` sums `TParameter<double>`
    92	  objects, inflating the detector-geometry nucleon constant by 12×. The
    93	  Python extraction uses the fixed tracker-geometry constant 3.2353e30.
    94	- Old pre-fix merged OmniFold ROOT files may carry summed
    95	  `pTmu_fiducial_nucleons` metadata — treat as untrustworthy.
    96	- **`hadd` 100 GB TTree trap (large dump-all / `_universes_full` merges):**
    97	  plain `hadd -f` ABORTS when the merged `mc_signal_reco`/`mc_truth_denom`
    98	  trees exceed ROOT's default 100 GB `TTree::fgMaxTreeSize`. ROOT rolls over
    99	  to a continuation file (`..._1.root`) mid-merge and the `TFileMerger`
   100	  fatals: *"Output file ... has been deleted (likely due to a TTree larger
   101	  than 100Gb)"* → SIGABRT (exit 134), leaving a ~94 GB partial **missing the
   102	  `data` + `mc_background` trees** (they merge last). Hit on the 2D dump-all
   103	  (~119 GB) and the 3D dump-all (~120 GB), same 94 GB signature both times.
   104	  `hadd` has no flag to raise the limit, so use the Python `TFileMerger` that
   105	  bumps `TTree::SetMaxTreeSize` to 300 GB instead:
   106	  `uq/hadd_universes_full.py OUT INPUT...` (generic; serves 2D and 3D), wired
   107	  in `sbatch_hadd_*_universes_full.sh` with `mem=64G`. The merge is fast-method
   108	  (basket copy, I/O-bound, ~7 min for 120 GB). **Never** bare-`hadd` a
   109	  `_universes_full` omnifile.
   110	- `runEventLoop.cpp` (baseline histogram path) is where
   111	  `pTmu_reweightedflux_integrated` is written;
   112	  `runEventLoopOmniFold.cpp` does **not** write the flux histogram. Use
   113	  `sbatch_runEventLoop_baseline_flux_array.sh` to regenerate per-playlist
   114	  baseline flux files, then `combine_flux_MEFHC.py` to build the
   115	  POT-weighted MEFHC flux (`baseline_flux/runEventLoopMC_MEFHC.root`).
   116	
   117	## 2D Python unfolding contract
   118	
   119	`2d-unfolding/unfold_2d_omnifold_unbinned.py` is the authoritative 2D unbinned
   120	OmniFold extraction path. Required invariants:
   121	
   122	1. **Mask measured data to phase space** (0 ≤ p_T ≤ 4.5, 1.5 ≤ p_|| ≤ 60)
   123	   before step-1 training.
   124	2. **Subtract background** — build non-negative reco-space target from
   125	   `data − bkg` and pass per-event `measured_weights` into
   126	   `ohf.omnifold(...)`.
   127	3. **Include signal fakes in the reco-side subtraction.** Fakes
   128	   (reco in phase space, truth out) appear in `mc_signal_reco` but are
   129	   filtered by `omnifold.py`'s `MC_pass_truth_mask`. Add their POT-scaled
   130	   reco weights into `hBkgReco2D` so both measured and MC reco sides are
   131	   fake-free.
   132	4. **`hUnfold2D` is a truth-space event yield.** Fill it with
   133	   `step2_weights * truth_w_in`, not raw `step2_weights`.
   134	5. **Do not divide `hUnfold2D` by `hEff2D`** when building `hXSec2D`.
   135	   OmniFold returns an efficiency-corrected truth spectrum on the
   136	   truth-selected sample. `hEff2D` is diagnostic only.
   137	6. **Closure mode** must restrict pseudo-data to `pass_reco & pass_truth`
   138	   and use `sig["w_reco"]` as `measured_weights` when `--use-weights` is
   139	   on, matching the `MCreco_weights` footing.
   140	7. **Full-stats sbatch must pass `--use-weights`.** The 1A validation runs
   141	   use it; the production path must match.
   142	
   143	## Bootstrap-replica workflow (`--bootstrap-seed N`)
   144	
   145	Per-event Poisson(1) weight bootstrap on data + MC jointly. Invariants:
   146	
   147	1. **Two independent sub-RNGs per replica** —
   148	   `np.random.default_rng(seed)` for data, `np.random.default_rng(seed +
   149	   10_000_000)` for MC. Data-stat and MC-stat are independent variance
   150	   sources; they must not share a draw or the covariance under-counts.
   151	2. **MC reco and MC truth weights ride a single per-event MC draw.**
   152	   `sig["w_truth"]` and `sig["w_reco"]` are multiplied by the *same*
   153	   per-event Poisson factor — they're two views of the same MC event and
   154	   must stay correlated within the event row. Using independent draws
   155	   for w_truth and w_reco would smear out the migration matrix.
   156	3. **`--bootstrap-seed` + `--closure` is coverage-toy mode only.**
   157	   Closure mode copies `sig["w_reco"]` into `measured_weights` before
   158	   the bootstrap multiply; adding independent data/MC Poisson draws
   159	   intentionally breaks the strict closure identity. Use this only for
   160	   pseudo-experiment coverage calibration, not for deterministic closure
   161	   tests.
   162	4. **Pin the GBT random_state too.** Each replica should pass both
   163	   `--bootstrap-seed N` and `--seed N` so ML stochasticity is removed
   164	   from the per-replica variance. The bootstrap variance and the
   165	   seedscan variance are *separable* uncorrelated components only when
   166	   each replica's GBT seed is pinned.
   167	5. **CV unfold = omit the flag.** Don't pass `--bootstrap-seed 0` and
   168	   call it the CV; seed=0 is a valid replica with a non-trivial Poisson
   169	   draw. The CV is the unflagged run.
   170	
   171	Driver: `uq/run_bootstrap_interactive.sh` runs N replicas inside an
   172	existing interactive allocation, batched WIDTH-wide ×
   173	(128/WIDTH)-threads. **Use WIDTH=1 on Perlmutter single-node interactive
   174	allocations.** The 2026-05-19 contention lesson (sklearn HistGBT
   175	bandwidth-bound at WIDTH≥2) has not been re-benchmarked for lgbm; even
   176	though lgbm scales fine to 128 threads on a single MEFHC unfold,
   177	WIDTH>1 packed onto one node is unmeasured. Until benchmarked, run
   178	sequential at full node width.
   179	
   180	Analyzer: `uq/analyze_uq.py` rolls N replica ROOTs into per-bin
   181	mean/std, 205×205 covariance on paper-reported bins, and 1D pT/pz
   182	projection covariances. The Cholesky PD check uses a small jitter
   183	fallback because the covariance is rank-deficient by construction
   184	whenever N ≤ n_reported (205); a meaningful PD test needs N >
   185	n_reported or pooling across replica sets. Quote the 205 paper-reported
   186	bins everywhere, not the full 14×16 grid.
   187	
   188	## Universe-covariance workflow (`--universe BAND:IDX`)
   189	
   190	Universe covariance is computed from per-universe deltas relative to a
   191	matched CV unfold. The CV must use the same omnifile, estimator,
   192	`--seed`, iteration count, `--use-weights` setting, and flux input as the
   193	universe sweep; only `--universe` is omitted. Mixing an exact-GBT
   194	production CV with lgbm universe outputs injects backend/seed baseline
   195	differences into every paired systematic covariance.
   196	
   197	For the full lateral+vertical MEFHC sweep:
   198	
   199	- Universe sweep:
   200	  `sbatch_unfold_2d_MEFHC_5iter_universes_full.sh` →
   201	  `uq/2d_xsec_MEFHC_5iter_lgbm_uni_full_<BAND>_<IDX>.root`.
   202	- Matched CV:
   203	  `sbatch_unfold_2d_MEFHC_5iter_universes_full_CV.sh` →
   204	  `uq/2d_xsec_MEFHC_5iter_lgbm_uni_full_CV.root`.
   205	- Rollup:
   206	  `sbatch_final_rollup_full.sh`, whose driver
   207	  `uq/final_rollup_full.sh` refuses to run without the matched full-CV
   208	  ROOT and archives any superseded baseline-mismatched full-rollup
   209	  artifacts before writing replacements.
   210	- Plots:
   211	  `uq_universe_band_pt.png` and `uq_universe_band_pz.png` show grouped
   212	  categories, not one line per universe band. Categories are Flux,
   213	  Models, Normalization, Statistical, Hadronic response, and Muon
   214	  reconstruction. The Statistical line appears when a bootstrap
   215	  covariance is supplied to `analyze_universes.py`.
   216	- Paper-Fig.-6/7-style fractional uncertainty projections are made with
   217	  `uq/plot_uncertainty_fig6_7_style.py`. Unlike the quick grouped sigma
   218	  diagnostic in `analyze_universes.py`, this script projects the full
   219	  205x205 covariance exactly, `C_1D = P C_2D P^T`, before dividing by
   220	  the reported-bin 1D central value. It writes
   221	  `uq/universe_stage2_MEFHC_full/MEFHC_fig6_7_uncertainty_{pz,pt}.png`
   222	  and a numeric summary text file. ML covariance is included in the
   223	  total when available, but is drawn only when it exceeds the configured
   224	  visibility threshold.
   225	
   226	All systematic bands use the MAT covariance convention implemented by
   227	`MnvVertErrorBand::CalcCovMx`: center universes on their universe mean and
   228	normalize by biased `1/N`. For a +/- pair this is the outer product of the
   229	half-difference; a common endpoint displacement from CV is reported separately
   230	rather than counted as variance. Multi-universe bands use the same formula. Inverse-cov
   231	diagnostics for an ours-only covariance are expected to be fragile when
   232	finite universe ensembles underspan the 205 reported-bin space; quote
   233	direct per-bin pulls and truncated-mode chi2 alongside any pseudo-inverse
   234	chi2.
   235	
   236	`--universe BAND:IDX` on a dump-all omnifile remains limited to CV event-loop
   237	support. When shifted selection can move events into or out of the sample, use
   238	per-playlist `MNV101_ACTIVE_UNIVERSE=BAND:IDX`, merge the ordinary trees, and
   239	require migration metadata plus finite-support signal/denominator closure. The
   240	five kinematic bands are BeamAngleX/Y, MuonResolution, and
   241	Muon_Energy_MINERvA/MINOS; MinosEfficiency and GEANT are weight-only.
   242	
   243	## Flux and normalization
   244	
   245	- The paper scalar `6.32e-8 /cm²/POT` is **not** the same quantity as the
   246	  flux normalization term used by this code path. Substituting it blows up
   247	  the total xsec by ~13.8×.
   248	- Outputs embed `hFlux_pt` and `fluxSource` so later auditing doesn't
   249	  depend on reopening an external ROOT file.
   250	- Playlist-dependent flux differences are a ~0.2 % effect; not a dominant
   251	  systematic.
   252	
   253	## Paper / ancillary comparison
   254	
   255	- No HepData entry. Authoritative target: `2d-unfolding/minerva_paper_anc/`.
   256	- Use `bin_mapping.txt`, not the axis labels on the ancillary TH2D.
   257	- `pzb=1` = first p_|| bin, 1.5 < p_|| < 2.0 GeV/c (the fragile low-p_||
   258	  MINOS range-out regime).
   259	
   260	## `IsMinosMatchMuon` (current state; our edit 2026-04-25)
   261	
   262	The upstream MINERvA-101 tutorial `main` now does a real MINOS match:
   263	
   264	```cpp
   265	virtual bool IsMinosMatchMuon() const {
   266	  return GetIsMinosMatchTrack() == 1;
   267	}
   268	```
   269	
   270	Our `CVUniverse.h:107` does the same `isMinosMatchTrack==1` match and adds the
   271	`minos_trk_is_ok==1` fit-quality bit:
   272	
   273	```cpp
   274	return GetInt("isMinosMatchTrack") == 1 &&
   275	       GetInt(GetAnaToolName() + "_minos_trk_is_ok") == 1;
   276	```
   277	
   278	(An early tutorial revision instead used `has_interaction_vertex==1`, an
   279	educational stub true for essentially every event in the pre-selected
   280	AnaTuple, which biased the low-p_|| cross section low. Our 2026-04-25 edit
   281	predates the upstream tutorial's own fix; both now enforce the real match.)
   282	
   283	This requires a MINOS-matched muon track with a passing MINOS fit.
   284	AnaTuple branches verified on `MasterAnaDev_mc_AnaTuple_run00110000_Playlist.root`:
   285	`isMinosMatchTrack` (Int_t) ∈ {−1, 1}; `MasterAnaDev_minos_trk_is_ok`
   286	(Bool_t) ∈ {0, 1}.
   287	
   288	Any pre-patch event-loop outputs (`runEventLoopOmniFold_*.root` from before
   289	2026-04-25) include the looser selection and must be regenerated before
   290	they can be compared to paper numbers.
   291	
   292	**Note**: this fix removed a real selection bug (background rate dropped
   293	from ~10 % to 0.35 %, matching the paper's ~0.2 %), but the residual
   294	low-p_|| sum-ratio gradient (0.6 at p_||=1.5–2 GeV/c rising to ~1.0
   295	above p_||=20 GeV/c) **persists** after the fix. The gradient is not
   296	caused by MINOS-match selection; it is most likely a MINOS geometric
   297	acceptance / range-out efficiency that the MINERvA-101 tutorial path
   298	does not implement.
   299	
   300	**Fixability assessment (2026-06-10, KNOWN_ISSUES #5)**: the
   301	`MINOSEfficiencyReweighter` (intensity-dependent data/MC matching
   302	efficiency, few-%) IS already applied in our MnvTune stack
   303	(`runEventLoopOmniFold.cpp:1113`) — far too small to explain the
   304	gradient. The candidate was the official MasterAnaDev muon-quality
   305	selection the tutorial path omits. Scoping found dead-time already in
   306	the preCuts (`NoDeadtime(1)`) and `minos_trk_fit_pass` implied by the
   307	patched IsMinosMatchMuon (100% of matched events), leaving
   308	`minos_trk_quality==1` (23.5% of matched MC is quality-2) and the
   309	curvature-significance cut.
   310	
   311	**Diagnostic RESULT (2026-06-10, job 54280253,
   312	`minos_quality_diagnostic.py` → `products/minos_quality_diagnostic.png`)
   313	— quality cuts ACQUITTED.** Conditional efficiency of the added cuts
   314	among base-selected events (1A AnaTuples, 1.66M MC / 158k data):
   315	eff_data/eff_MC = 1.03–1.05 at p_MINOS 1–2.5 GeV/c vs 1.06–1.14 at
   316	10–60 (quality and quality+curvature alike). Closing the 0.6→1.0
   317	gradient needed ~1.67 at low p falling to 1.0 — the observed double
   318	ratio is far too small and has the wrong shape. Data is uniformly MORE
   319	efficient than MC for these cuts, so applying them cannot produce a
   320	low-p_|| data deficit at all. (Side finding: `minos_trk_eqp_qp` is the
   321	already-fractional q/p error — the /qp variant removes >99% of
   322	high-momentum events and is the wrong reading.) The gradient therefore
   323	remains attributed to MINOS acceptance/efficiency modeling upstream of
   324	selection (or generator rate mismodeling); its net effect inside the
   325	published phase space is bounded by the 2D paper reproduction (integral
   326	ratio 1.011), and the FPS p_||<1.5 region carries tier-2 flagging.
   327	
   328	## Output hygiene
   329	
   330	- Preserve known-bad but diagnostically useful files under an explicit
   331	  `archive_*/debug/` name before a rerun overwrites a canonical path.
   332	- Never leave invalidated outputs at canonical production paths once a
   333	  corrected rerun is complete.
   334	
   335	## 3D OmniFold extension (Workstream C)
   336	
   337	The 3D available-energy extension (`d³σ/(dp_T dp_‖ dE_avail)`) lives in the
   338	sibling `../3d-unfolding/` and **reuses this contract**: its driver
   339	`unfold_3d_omnifold_unbinned.py` does `import unfold_2d_omnifold_unbinned` and
   340	calls the 2D helpers, so everything above — POT scaling, `TRACKER_FIDUCIAL_N_NUCLEONS`,
   341	the `in_truth_phase_space` θ_μ<20° gate, the per-p_T flux convention, the
   342	`hXSec2D` naming for `compare_to_paper_fullcov.py` — applies unchanged. 3D-only
   343	invariants:
   344	
   345	- **Eavail axis defs** (arXiv:2312.16631 Eq. 4): truth `GetEAvailableTrue()`,
   346	  reco `NewEavail()` (tracker+ECAL ×1.17), added standalone to `CVUniverse.h`
   347	  (the MAT calculator headers redefine `GetVertex()`). MeV→GeV in the branches.
   348	- **Eavail binning catch bin**: the top edge must be large (default 100 GeV) so
   349	  the Eavail-marginal captures the full recoil tail — `Σ_k xsec·ΔE_k = 2D` only
   350	  if every truth event lands in a bin. A truncated top edge breaks the anchor.
   351	- **CV-only**: the 3D driver does NOT carry the universe / alt-model / bootstrap
   352	  machinery; that is the deferred 3D-UQ campaign.
   353	- **`compare_to_paper_fullcov.py` from 3d-unfolding/**: always pass
   354	  `--out-prefix`, or the default clobbers the tracked 2D `MEFHC_5iter_pull_full.png`.
   355	
   356	Its own docs (mirroring this triad): `../3d-unfolding/3D_OMNIFOLD_STATUS.md`
   357	(dashboard), `../3d-unfolding/3D_OMNIFOLD_RUN_LOG.md` (chronology),
   358	`../3d-unfolding/README.md` (orientation + how-to-run).
   359	
   360	## Documentation split

1	#!/usr/bin/env python3
     2	"""Universe-covariance rollup for the ND (4D q3) OmniFold UQ campaign.
     3	
     4	ND analogue of ../3d-unfolding/uq_3d/analyze_universes_3d.py. Reads the flat
     5	hXSecND_flat (C-order ravel over the nd shape) from a CV unfold and the per-(band,idx)
     6	universe unfolds produced by `unfold_nd_omnifold_unbinned.py --universe BAND:IDX`, and
     7	builds the block-summed systematic covariance (per-band C_band = (1/N)sum (X-<X>)(X-<X>)^T,
     8	MAT-conformant), an optional flat normalization band, and an optional block-sum with extra
     9	(stat/ML) covariances. Reported bins = CV>0.
    10	
    11	  python analyze_universes_nd.py --cv products/4d/xsec_4d_MEFHC_5iter_lgbm.root \
    12	      --glob 'uq_4d/universe_sweep/4d_xsec_*_uni_full_*.root' \
    13	      --add-norm 0.014 --outdir uq_4d/universe_stage2_4d/
    14	"""
    15	import argparse
    16	import glob
    17	import os
    18	import re
    19	from collections import defaultdict
    20	
    21	import numpy as np
    22	import ROOT
    23	
    24	ROOT.gROOT.SetBatch(True)
    25	UNI_RE = re.compile(r".*_uni(?:_full)?_(?P<band>[A-Za-z0-9_]+?)_(?P<idx>\d+)\.root$")
    26	
    27	CATEGORY_ORDER = ["Flux", "Models", "Normalization", "Hadronic response",
    28	                  "Muon reconstruction"]
    29	
    30	
    31	def category_for_band(band):
    32	    c = band[len("full_"):] if band.startswith("full_") else band
    33	    if c == "Flux":
    34	        return "Flux"
    35	    if c == "__Normalization_flat":
    36	        return "Normalization"
    37	    if c.startswith("Fr") or c.startswith("MFP_") or c.startswith("GEANT_"):
    38	        return "Hadronic response"
    39	    if (c.startswith("Muon_") or c.startswith("BeamAngle") or
    40	            c in {"MuonResolution", "MinosEfficiency"}):
    41	        return "Muon reconstruction"
    42	    return "Models"
    43	
    44	
    45	def load_flat(path):
    46	    f = ROOT.TFile.Open(path)
    47	    if not f or f.IsZombie():
    48	        raise SystemExit(f"[FAIL] cannot open {path}")
    49	    h = f.Get("hXSecND_flat")
    50	    if not h:
    51	        raise SystemExit(f"[FAIL] hXSecND_flat missing in {path}")
    52	    a = np.array([h.GetBinContent(i + 1) for i in range(h.GetNbinsX())])
    53	    f.Close()
    54	    return a
    55	
    56	
    57	def main():
    58	    ap = argparse.ArgumentParser()
    59	    ap.add_argument("--cv", required=True)
    60	    ap.add_argument("--glob", required=True)
    61	    ap.add_argument("--add-norm", type=float, default=0.0)
    62	    ap.add_argument("--bootstrap-cov", nargs="+", default=None, metavar="ROOT:HIST")
    63	    ap.add_argument("--outdir", default="uq_4d/universe_stage2_4d")
    64	    ap.add_argument("--out-root", default="uq_universe_4d_covariance.root")
    65	    args = ap.parse_args()
    66	    os.makedirs(args.outdir, exist_ok=True)
    67	
    68	    cv = load_flat(args.cv)
    69	    print(f"[INFO] CV flat nbins={cv.size}")
    70	    paths = sorted(glob.glob(args.glob))
    71	    if not paths:
    72	        raise SystemExit(f"[FAIL] no universe files matched {args.glob}")
    73	
    74	    by_band = defaultdict(list)
    75	    for p in paths:
    76	        m = UNI_RE.match(os.path.basename(p))
    77	        if not m:
    78	            print(f"  [skip] {p}")
    79	            continue
    80	        by_band[m.group("band")].append((int(m.group("idx")), load_flat(p) - cv))
    81	    print(f"[INFO] {len(paths)} universe files across {len(by_band)} bands")
    82	
    83	    rep = cv > 0
    84	    n_rep = int(rep.sum())
    85	    print(f"[INFO] reported bins = {n_rep} of {cv.size}")
    86	    cv_rep = cv[rep]
    87	
    88	    band_cov, total = {}, np.zeros((n_rep, n_rep))
    89	    for band, entries in sorted(by_band.items()):
    90	        D = np.stack([d[rep] for _, d in sorted(entries)], axis=0)
    91	        if D.shape[0] < 2:
    92	            print(f"  [skip] {band}: {D.shape[0]} universe(s)")
    93	            continue
    94	        Z = D - D.mean(axis=0, keepdims=True)
    95	        cov = (Z.T @ Z) / D.shape[0]
    96	        band_cov[band] = cov
    97	        total += cov
    98	        diag = np.sqrt(np.maximum(np.diag(cov), 0))
    99	        with np.errstate(divide="ignore", invalid="ignore"):
   100	            rel = np.where(cv_rep > 0, diag / cv_rep, 0)
   101	        print(f"  [{band:24s} N={D.shape[0]:3d}] sqrt-tr={np.sqrt(max(np.trace(cov),0)):.3e} "
   102	              f"med rel={100*np.median(rel):.2f}%")
   103	
   104	    if args.add_norm > 0:
   105	        v = args.add_norm * cv_rep
   106	        nc = np.outer(v, v)
   107	        band_cov["__Normalization_flat"] = nc
   108	        total += nc
   109	        print(f"  [__Normalization_flat sigma={args.add_norm}] sqrt-tr={np.sqrt(np.trace(nc)):.3e}")
   110	
   111	    def cond_rank(m, rc=1e-12):
   112	        ev = np.linalg.eigvalsh(0.5 * (m + m.T))
   113	        pos = ev[ev > ev.max() * rc] if ev.size else ev
   114	        return int(pos.size)
   115	
   116	    tdiag = np.sqrt(np.maximum(np.diag(total), 0))
   117	    with np.errstate(divide="ignore", invalid="ignore"):
   118	        trel = np.where(cv_rep > 0, tdiag / cv_rep, 0)
   119	    print(f"\n[TOTAL syst 4D] sqrt-trace={np.sqrt(max(np.trace(total),0)):.3e} "
   120	          f"rank={cond_rank(total)}/{n_rep} median rel={100*np.median(trel):.2f}% "
   121	          f"p84={100*np.percentile(trel,84):.2f}%")
   122	
   123	    combined = None
   124	    if args.bootstrap_cov:
   125	        combined = total.copy()
   126	        for spec in args.bootstrap_cov:
   127	            bp, _, bh = spec.partition(":")
   128	            bh = bh or "hCov_stat_reported"
   129	            bf = ROOT.TFile.Open(bp)
   130	            hh = bf.Get(bh)
   131	            nb = hh.GetNbinsX()
   132	            extra = np.array([[hh.GetBinContent(i + 1, j + 1) for j in range(nb)]
   133	                              for i in range(nb)])
   134	            bf.Close()
   135	            if extra.shape != total.shape:
   136	                raise SystemExit(f"[FAIL] {spec} shape {extra.shape} != {total.shape}")
   137	            combined += extra
   138	            print(f"  [+ {bp}:{bh} sqrt-tr={np.sqrt(max(np.trace(extra),0)):.3e}]")
   139	        cdiag = np.sqrt(np.maximum(np.diag(combined), 0))
   140	        with np.errstate(divide="ignore", invalid="ignore"):
   141	            crel = np.where(cv_rep > 0, cdiag / cv_rep, 0)
   142	        print(f"[COMBINED 4D] sqrt-trace={np.sqrt(max(np.trace(combined),0)):.3e} "
   143	              f"rank={cond_rank(combined)}/{n_rep} median rel={100*np.median(crel):.2f}%")
   144	
   145	    out = os.path.join(args.outdir, args.out_root)
   146	    rf = ROOT.TFile.Open(out, "RECREATE")
   147	
   148	    def wcov(name, mat):
   149	        n = mat.shape[0]
   150	        h = ROOT.TH2D(name, name, n, 0, n, n, 0, n)
   151	        for i in range(n):
   152	            for j in range(n):
   153	                h.SetBinContent(i + 1, j + 1, float(mat[i, j]))
   154	        h.Write()
   155	
   156	    wcov("hCov_universe4d_total", total)
   157	    for b, c in band_cov.items():
   158	        wcov(f"hCov_universe4d_{b}", c)
   159	    if combined is not None:
   160	        wcov("hCov_combined4d_total", combined)
   161	    rf.Close()
   162	    print(f"[wrote] {out}")
   163	
   164	    with open(os.path.join(args.outdir, "uq_universe_4d_summary.txt"), "w") as fh:
   165	        fh.write(f"CV: {args.cv}\nglob: {args.glob}\nreported bins: {n_rep}/{cv.size}\n")
   166	        fh.write(f"total syst sqrt-trace={np.sqrt(max(np.trace(total),0)):.4e} "
   167	                 f"median rel={100*np.median(trel):.3f}%\n")
   168	        grp = defaultdict(float)
   169	        for b, c in band_cov.items():
   170	            grp[category_for_band(b)] += np.sqrt(max(np.trace(c), 0))
   171	        for cat in CATEGORY_ORDER:
   172	            if grp[cat] > 0:
   173	                fh.write(f"  {cat:22s} sum sqrt-trace={grp[cat]:.3e}\n")
   174	    print(f"[wrote] {os.path.join(args.outdir, 'uq_universe_4d_summary.txt')}")
   175	
   176	
   177	if __name__ == "__main__":
   178	    main()
     1	#!/usr/bin/env python3
     2	"""P4-FPS validation (Agent C): the selection-complete active scalar FPS lateral covariance
     3	vs the support-limited (dump-all-bank) FPS lateral block. Self-contained FPS sibling of
     4	p4_validate_active_lateral.py -- same matrix gates + active-vs-support sqrt-trace/per-bin
     5	comparison, but reads the FPS `hCov_universe4d_<band>` block naming (analyze_universes_nd.py
     6	writes 4d-tagged names for every ND build, FPS included) and does NOT re-do the migration
     7	census (audit_merged_fps.py already gates the hadd-summed census on the merged endpoints).
     8	
     9	  p4_validate_active_lateral_fps.py \
    10	    --active active_universe_5d/fps/covariance/active_scalar_lateral_fps_cov.root:hCov_universe4d_total \
    11	    --support uq_fps/corrected/universe_stage2_fps/uq_universe_fps_covariance_combined.root \
    12	    --band-prefix hCov_universe4d_ \
    13	    --out active_universe_5d/fps/covariance/p4_active_lateral_fps_summary.json
    14	"""
    15	import argparse, json, os, sys
    16	import numpy as np
    17	import ROOT
    18	
    19	ROOT.gROOT.SetBatch(True)
    20	# the 5 selection-complete lateral bands (muon angle / resolution / energy scales); the
    21	# support-limited comparator is the sum of these per-band blocks in the FPS combined ROOT.
    22	BANDS = ["BeamAngleX", "BeamAngleY", "MuonResolution",
    23	         "Muon_Energy_MINERvA", "Muon_Energy_MINOS"]
    24	
    25	
    26	def load_th2(spec):
    27	    path, key = spec.rsplit(":", 1)
    28	    f = ROOT.TFile.Open(path)
    29	    if not f or f.IsZombie():
    30	        raise SystemExit(f"cannot open {path}")
    31	    h = f.Get(key)
    32	    if not h:
    33	        raise SystemExit(f"missing key {key} in {path}")
    34	    n = h.GetNbinsX()
    35	    C = np.empty((n, n))
    36	    for i in range(n):
    37	        for j in range(n):
    38	            C[i, j] = h.GetBinContent(i + 1, j + 1)
    39	    f.Close()
    40	    return C
    41	
    42	
    43	def load_th2_key(path, key):
    44	    f = ROOT.TFile.Open(path)
    45	    if not f or f.IsZombie():
    46	        return None
    47	    h = f.Get(key)
    48	    if not h:
    49	        f.Close(); return None
    50	    n = h.GetNbinsX()
    51	    C = np.empty((n, n))
    52	    for i in range(n):
    53	        for j in range(n):
    54	            C[i, j] = h.GetBinContent(i + 1, j + 1)
    55	    f.Close()
    56	    return C
    57	
    58	
    59	def mat_gates(C, tag):
    60	    r = {}
    61	    r["shape"] = list(C.shape)
    62	    r["all_finite"] = bool(np.all(np.isfinite(C)))
    63	    asym = np.max(np.abs(C - C.T)) / max(1e-300, np.max(np.abs(C)))
    64	    r["rel_asymmetry"] = float(asym)
    65	    Cs = 0.5 * (C + C.T)
    66	    ev = np.linalg.eigvalsh(Cs)
    67	    r["min_eig"] = float(ev[0]); r["max_eig"] = float(ev[-1])
    68	    r["min_over_max_eig"] = float(ev[0] / max(1e-300, ev[-1]))
    69	    r["psd"] = bool(ev[0] >= -1e-12 * abs(ev[-1]))
    70	    d = np.diag(C)
    71	    r["diag_finite_nonneg"] = bool(np.all(np.isfinite(d)) and np.all(d >= -1e-30))
    72	    r["sqrt_trace"] = float(np.sqrt(np.clip(np.trace(Cs), 0, None)))
    73	    r["n_reported"] = int(np.sum(d > 0))
    74	    print(f"[{tag}] shape={r['shape']} finite={r['all_finite']} "
    75	          f"asym={asym:.2e} min/max_eig={r['min_over_max_eig']:.2e} PSD={r['psd']} "
    76	          f"sqrt_tr={r['sqrt_trace']:.4e}")
    77	    return r
    78	
    79	
    80	def main():
    81	    ap = argparse.ArgumentParser()
    82	    ap.add_argument("--active", required=True, help="ROOT:key of the active FPS lateral total cov")
    83	    ap.add_argument("--support", required=True, help="uq_universe_fps_covariance_combined.root")
    84	    ap.add_argument("--band-prefix", default="hCov_universe4d_",
    85	                    help="per-band block key prefix in the support ROOT")
    86	    ap.add_argument("--out", required=True)
    87	    a = ap.parse_args()
    88	
    89	    out = {"gates": {}, "active": {}, "support": {}, "comparison": {}}
    90	    fails = []
    91	
    92	    Cact = load_th2(a.active)
    93	    out["active"] = mat_gates(Cact, "active(FPS selection-complete lateral)")
    94	
    95	    # hard gates on the active P4-FPS product
    96	    for g in ("all_finite", "psd", "diag_finite_nonneg"):
    97	        if not out["active"][g]:
    98	            fails.append(f"active {g}")
    99	    if out["active"]["rel_asymmetry"] > 1e-9:
   100	        fails.append("active asymmetry >1e-9")
   101	
   102	    # support-limited block = sum of the 5 kinematic per-band covs in the FPS combined ROOT
   103	    Csup = None; present = []
   104	    for b in BANDS:
   105	        cb = load_th2_key(a.support, f"{a.band_prefix}{b}")
   106	        if cb is None:
   107	            print(f"[support] band {b} key '{a.band_prefix}{b}' absent"); continue
   108	        present.append(b)
   109	        Csup = cb if Csup is None else Csup + cb
   110	    out["support"]["bands_present"] = present
   111	    if Csup is None:
   112	        fails.append("no support-limited lateral bands found (check --band-prefix)")
   113	    else:
   114	        out["support"].update(mat_gates(Csup, "support(sum of 5 FPS bands)"))
   115	        if Csup.shape == Cact.shape:
   116	            st_a = out["active"]["sqrt_trace"]; st_s = out["support"]["sqrt_trace"]
   117	            da = np.sqrt(np.clip(np.diag(Cact), 0, None))
   118	            ds = np.sqrt(np.clip(np.diag(Csup), 0, None))
   119	            m = ds > 0
   120	            ratio = np.divide(da, ds, out=np.zeros_like(da), where=m)
   121	            out["comparison"] = {
   122	                "sqrt_trace_active": st_a,
   123	                "sqrt_trace_support": st_s,
   124	                "sqrt_trace_ratio_active_over_support": float(st_a / max(1e-300, st_s)),
   125	                "per_bin_sigma_ratio_median": float(np.median(ratio[m])),
   126	                "per_bin_sigma_ratio_p16": float(np.percentile(ratio[m], 16)),
   127	                "per_bin_sigma_ratio_p84": float(np.percentile(ratio[m], 84)),
   128	                "n_bins_active_gt_support": int(np.sum(da > ds)),
   129	                "n_bins_common": int(np.sum(m)),
   130	            }
   131	            print(f"[compare] sqrt_tr active={st_a:.4e} support={st_s:.4e} "
   132	                  f"ratio={st_a/max(1e-300,st_s):.3f}; per-bin median ratio "
   133	                  f"{out['comparison']['per_bin_sigma_ratio_median']:.3f}")
   134	        else:
   135	            fails.append(f"active/support shape mismatch {Cact.shape} vs {Csup.shape}")
   136	
   137	    out["gates"]["fails"] = fails
   138	    out["gates"]["result"] = "PASS" if not fails else "FAIL"
   139	    os.makedirs(os.path.dirname(a.out), exist_ok=True)
   140	    with open(a.out, "w") as fh:
   141	        json.dump(out, fh, indent=2)
   142	    print(f"\nRESULT {out['gates']['result']}" + ("" if not fails else " :: " + "; ".join(fails)))
   143	    print(f"summary -> {a.out}")
   144	    sys.exit(0 if not fails else 1)
   145	
   146	
   147	if __name__ == "__main__":
   148	    main()
     1	#!/usr/bin/env python3
     2	"""P6-FPS FINAL lateral adoption (Agent C): swap the support-limited FPS lateral block for the
     3	selection-complete active scalar lateral block in the combined FPS covariance, by PURE COMPONENT
     4	SUM (never subtraction -- the D5 lesson: subtracting a DIFFERENT-estimator block breaks PSD; here
     5	we simply rebuild the combined by summing PSD pieces).
     6	
     7	    C_comb_active = SUM_{band in universe4d blocks EXCEPT total and the 5 lateral} C_band   (incl.
     8	                    __Normalization_flat, MinosEfficiency, and every vertical/model/hadronic band)
     9	                  + C_stat (external uq_cov_stat_fps.root:hCov_statfps_reported)
    10	                  + C_ML   (external uq_cov_mlsplit_fps.root:hCov_mlsplitfps_reported)
    11	                  + C_active_lateral (active_scalar_lateral_fps_cov.root:hCov_universe4d_total)
    12	
    13	Writes an intermediate combined ROOT that (a) carries the rebuilt hCov_combined4d_total, (b) copies
    14	EVERY original per-band hCov_universe4d_<band> block through unchanged (so the downstream
    15	adopt_unified_4d.py can still read the 13 vertical per-band carriers), and (c) records the active
    16	lateral total under hCov_universe4d_active_lateral_total. A subtraction-based cross-check
    17	(C_comb_orig - SUM(5 support lateral) + C_active) is reported for consistency but is NOT the product.
    18	
    19	Then run the unified-throw adopt on THIS file for the publishable covariance:
    20	  python adopt_unified_4d.py --uthrow uq_fps/corrected/unified_throw_cov_fps.root \
    21	      --combined <this --out> --prod uq_fps/universe_sweep/fps2d_xsec_MEFHC_5iter_lgbm_uni_full_CV.root \
    22	      --out uq_fps/corrected/universe_stage2_fps/uq_universe_fps_covariance_combined_uthrow_activelat.root
    23	
    24	  python adopt_active_lateral_fps.py    # defaults below
    25	"""
    26	import argparse
    27	import numpy as np
    28	import ROOT
    29	
    30	ROOT.gROOT.SetBatch(True)
    31	LATERAL = ["BeamAngleX", "BeamAngleY", "MuonResolution",
    32	           "Muon_Energy_MINERvA", "Muon_Energy_MINOS"]
    33	
    34	
    35	def _th2(h):
    36	    n = h.GetNbinsX()
    37	    C = np.empty((n, n))
    38	    for i in range(n):
    39	        for j in range(n):
    40	            C[i, j] = h.GetBinContent(i + 1, j + 1)
    41	    return C
    42	
    43	
    44	def _load_key(path, key, required=True):
    45	    f = ROOT.TFile.Open(path)
    46	    if not f or f.IsZombie():
    47	        raise SystemExit(f"[FAIL] cannot open {path}")
    48	    h = f.Get(key)
    49	    if not h:
    50	        f.Close()
    51	        if required:
    52	            raise SystemExit(f"[FAIL] missing {key} in {path}")
    53	        return None
    54	    C = _th2(h)
    55	    f.Close()
    56	    return C
    57	
    58	
    59	def main():
    60	    ap = argparse.ArgumentParser()
    61	    ap.add_argument("--combined",
    62	                    default="uq_fps/corrected/universe_stage2_fps/uq_universe_fps_covariance_combined.root")
    63	    ap.add_argument("--active",
    64	                    default="active_universe_5d/fps/covariance/active_scalar_lateral_fps_cov.root:hCov_universe4d_total")
    65	    ap.add_argument("--stat", default="uq_fps/corrected/uq_cov_stat_fps.root:hCov_statfps_reported")
    66	    ap.add_argument("--ml", default="uq_fps/corrected/uq_cov_mlsplit_fps.root:hCov_mlsplitfps_reported")
    67	    ap.add_argument("--out",
    68	                    default="uq_fps/corrected/universe_stage2_fps/uq_universe_fps_covariance_combined_activelat.root")
    69	    a = ap.parse_args()
    70	
    71	    # active lateral (selection-complete)

     1	#!/usr/bin/env python3
     2	"""Adopt the rigorous 4D unified-throw systematic into the published combined 4D covariance.
     3	
     4	The unified throw (uq_4d/unified_throw_cov_4d.root, 160 joint re-unfolds of the 12 knob bands
     5	+ flux) measures the per-bin systematic variance INCLUDING the nonlinear cross-band term the
     6	block-sum drops. But with 160 throws << 4830 bins, C_unified is rank-160 -- a noisy estimate
     7	of the full 4830x4830 matrix. Swapping the whole matrix in directly breaks positive-definiteness
     8	(verified: 2285 negative eigenvalues, most-negative = -1.25% of max). So we adopt the throw's
     9	fractional MAGNITUDE inflation (per-bin variance, which converges fast and carries the nonlinear
    10	cross-term) and transfer it onto the SWEEP's own vertical block -- exactly the engine-independent
    11	fractional-transfer logic used for the PET lateral band (task 15). Using the sweep's block as the
    12	carrier (not the bank block) is what keeps the result PSD: the throw's own bank block-sum and the
    13	sweep block-sum are two DIFFERENT estimators, so subtracting the bank block from the sweep-based
    14	combined leaves uncancelled negative directions; subtracting the SWEEP vertical block does not.
    15	
    16	    g_i = sqrt( max(sigma_uni_i^2, sigma_blockbank_i^2) ) / sigma_blockbank_i   >= 1   (from the throw)
    17	    C_vert_adopt = G C_vert_sweep G              (G = diag(g);  PSD, sweep correlation preserved)
    18	    C_combined_new = (C_combined_old - C_vert_sweep) + C_vert_adopt
    19	                   = (C_other_bands + C_stat + C_ML) + G C_vert_sweep G        <- PSD by construction
    20	
    21	C_vert_sweep = sum of the 13 vertical per-band covs (12 knobs + Flux) the throw covers, taken
    22	from the combined file's per-band hCov_universe4d_<band> (so C_comb - C_vert_sweep is a sum of
    23	PSD pieces from the same sweep). The conservative max() never UNDER-covers relative to the block
    24	baseline in the ~50% of (noisy) bins where the throw dips below block; it inflates where the
    25	nonlinear cross-term is real (the high-pT / low-E_avail corner, top 1% of bins = 78% of excess).
    26	
    27	  python adopt_unified_4d.py        # writes uq_4d/.../uq_universe_4d_covariance_combined_uthrow.root
    28	"""
    29	import argparse
    30	import sys
    31	
    32	import numpy as np
    33	
    34	_REPO = "/pscratch/sd/j/josephrb/MINERvA-OmniFold"
    35	for _p in (f"{_REPO}/2d-unfolding", f"{_REPO}/nd-unfolding"):
    36	    if _p not in sys.path:
    37	        sys.path.insert(0, _p)
    38	
    39	
    40	def _th2(h):
    41	    nx, ny = h.GetNbinsX(), h.GetNbinsY()
    42	    b = np.frombuffer(h.GetArray(), dtype=np.float64, count=(nx + 2) * (ny + 2)).reshape(ny + 2, nx + 2)
    43	    return b[1:ny + 1, 1:nx + 1].T.copy()
    44	
    45	
    46	def main():
    47	    import ROOT
    48	    ap = argparse.ArgumentParser()
    49	    ap.add_argument("--uthrow", default="uq_4d/unified_throw_cov_4d.root")
    50	    ap.add_argument("--combined", default="uq_4d/universe_stage2_4d/uq_universe_4d_covariance_combined.root")
    51	    ap.add_argument("--prod", default="products/4d/xsec_4d_MEFHC_5iter_lgbm.root")
    52	    ap.add_argument("--out", default="uq_4d/universe_stage2_4d/uq_universe_4d_covariance_combined_uthrow.root")
    53	    ap.add_argument("--cv-centered", action="store_true",
    54	                    help="F7 CV-centered variant: add per-bin joint mean_shift^2 to the unified "
    55	                         "variance (default is mean-centered, diag(C_unified) only). Parity with "
    56	                         "adopt_unified_5d.py --cv-centered.")
    57	    args = ap.parse_args()
    58	
    59	    # the 13 vertical bands the unified throw covers (12 knobs + Flux)
    60	    VERT_BANDS = ["2p2h", "CCQEPauliSupViaKF", "FrAbs_pi", "FrElas_N", "HighQ2", "LowQ2",
    61	                  "MaCCQE", "MaRES", "MFP_N", "MvRES", "Rvn2pi", "Rvp2pi", "Flux"]
    62	
    63	    fu = ROOT.TFile.Open(args.uthrow)
    64	    C_uni = _th2(fu.Get("C_unified"))
    65	    C_block = _th2(fu.Get("C_blocksum"))      # bank vertical block-sum (the throw's own comparator)
    66	    mean_shift2 = None
    67	    if args.cv_centered:
    68	        hms = fu.Get("hJointMeanShift")
    69	        if not hms:
    70	            raise SystemExit("[FAIL] --cv-centered needs hJointMeanShift in the unified-throw ROOT")
    71	        ms = np.array([hms.GetBinContent(i + 1) for i in range(hms.GetNbinsX())])
    72	        mean_shift2 = ms ** 2
    73	    fu.Close()
    74	    fc = ROOT.TFile.Open(args.combined)
    75	    C_comb = _th2(fc.Get("hCov_combined4d_total"))
    76	    # the sweep's own vertical block = sum of the 13 per-band covs (same estimator as C_comb)
    77	    C_vert_sweep = None
    78	    for b in VERT_BANDS:
    79	        h = fc.Get(f"hCov_universe4d_{b}")
    80	        if not h:
    81	            raise SystemExit(f"[FAIL] missing per-band cov hCov_universe4d_{b}")
    82	        cb = _th2(h)
    83	        C_vert_sweep = cb if C_vert_sweep is None else C_vert_sweep + cb
    84	    fc.Close()
    85	    fp = ROOT.TFile.Open(args.prod)
    86	    h = fp.Get("hXSecND_flat")
    87	    xfull = np.array([h.GetBinContent(i + 1) for i in range(h.GetNbinsX())])
    88	    fp.Close()
    89	    x = xfull[xfull > 0]
    90	    n = C_comb.shape[0]
    91	    assert C_uni.shape == C_block.shape == C_comb.shape == C_vert_sweep.shape == (n, n), "dim mismatch"
    92	
    93	    vu = np.clip(np.diag(C_uni), 0, None)
    94	    if mean_shift2 is not None:
    95	        assert mean_shift2.size == vu.size, f"mean_shift dim {mean_shift2.size} != unified dim {vu.size}"
    96	        vu = vu + mean_shift2                  # F7 CV-centered: variance + shift^2
    97	    vb = np.clip(np.diag(C_block), 0, None)    # bank vertical block (the throw's comparator)
    98	    # conservative per-bin inflation FACTOR measured by the throw (never below block baseline)
    99	    s_adopt = np.sqrt(np.maximum(vu, vb))
   100	    sb = np.sqrt(vb)
   101	    g = np.ones(n)
   102	    m = sb > 0
   103	    g[m] = s_adopt[m] / sb[m]                  # >= 1
   104	    G = g[:, None] * g[None, :]
   105	    # transfer the fractional inflation onto the SWEEP's vertical block (PSD-preserving carrier)
   106	    C_vert_adopt = G * C_vert_sweep            # G C_vert_sweep G
   107	    C_new = (C_comb - C_vert_sweep) + C_vert_adopt
   108	
   109	    # symmetrize against float drift
   110	    C_new = 0.5 * (C_new + C_new.T)
   111	
   112	    # --- verification ---
   113	    ev = np.linalg.eigvalsh(C_new)
   114	    do = np.sqrt(np.clip(np.diag(C_comb), 0, None)) / x
   115	    dn = np.sqrt(np.clip(np.diag(C_new), 0, None)) / x
   116	    print(f"[adopt] bins                = {n}  (reported)")
   117	    print(f"[adopt] inflation g: bins>1 = {(g > 1.0001).sum()} ({100*(g>1.0001).mean():.1f}%)  "
   118	          f"median={np.median(g):.3f}  max={g.max():.2f}")
   119	    print(f"[adopt] sqrt-trace  old combined = {np.sqrt(np.trace(C_comb)):.4e}")
   120	    print(f"[adopt] sqrt-trace  new combined = {np.sqrt(np.trace(C_new)):.4e}  "
   121	          f"(x{np.sqrt(np.trace(C_new)/np.trace(C_comb)):.3f})")
   122	    print(f"[adopt] median frac/bin  old={100*np.median(do):.2f}%   new={100*np.median(dn):.2f}%")
   123	    print(f"[adopt] PSD check: min eigenvalue = {ev[0]:.3e}  "
   124	          f"(neg: {(ev<0).sum()}; most-neg/max = {ev[0]/ev[-1]:.2e})")
   125	    if ev[0] < -1e-12 * ev[-1]:
   126	        raise SystemExit("[FAIL] adopted covariance is not PSD")
   127	    print("[adopt] PSD OK (PSD by construction: lateral+stat+ML + G C_block G)")
   128	
   129	    # --- write ---
   130	    fo = ROOT.TFile.Open(args.out, "RECREATE")
   131	    nb = n
   132	    hnew = ROOT.TH2D("hCov_combined4d_total_uthrow", "combined 4D cov (unified-throw adopted)",
   133	                     nb, 0, nb, nb, 0, nb)
   134	    for i in range(nb):
   135	        for j in range(nb):
   136	            hnew.SetBinContent(i + 1, j + 1, C_new[i, j])
   137	    hnew.Write()
   138	    hg = ROOT.TH1D("hInflation_g", "per-bin unified/block sigma inflation", nb, 0, nb)
   139	    for i in range(nb):
   140	        hg.SetBinContent(i + 1, g[i])
   141	    hg.Write()
   142	    ROOT.TParameter("double")("sqrt_tr_old", float(np.sqrt(np.trace(C_comb)))).Write()
   143	    ROOT.TParameter("double")("sqrt_tr_new", float(np.sqrt(np.trace(C_new)))).Write()
   144	    fo.Close()
   145	    print(f"[adopt] wrote {args.out}")
   146	
   147	
   148	if __name__ == "__main__":
   149	    main()

docs/analysis-note/sec_execsummary.tex:31:  The standard covariance adds matrices built for one systematic source at a time, which omits cross-source response terms. In MINERvA's \texttt{GetTotalErrorMatrix}, each named source contributes its own covariance, built with all other sources held nominal; the sum retains within-source bin--bin correlations but contains no cross-source terms. The corrected unified-throw test perturbs all reweightable sources simultaneously using actual asymmetric endpoints, one fixed estimator seed, and mean-centered throws; it reports the joint mean shift separately. The resulting 5D products remain candidates until the selection-complete lateral replacement lands (\S\ref{sec:uq-combine}).

   265	  rm/redoes truncated, skips complete); (4) gate: require 10/10 `--all` PASS BEFORE cov; then
   266	  LATERAL COV (analyze_universes_nd.py) -> validate -> swap -> adopt, all via srun into the fresh holder.
   267	  Do NOT start cov/validate/swap/adopt until fps_unfold_complete.py --all == 10/10.

     1	#!/usr/bin/env python3
     2	"""Completeness gate for FPS active-endpoint unfolds (Agent C). A wall-killed unfold leaves a
     3	present-but-TRUNCATED .root (ROOT auto-recovered on reopen, or the final hXSecND_flat/histograms
     4	never written) -- the exact failure that makes a naive `-s`/existence skip consume a bad file. This
     5	validates each output is COMPLETE so the driver never skips (and the P4 chain never ingests) a
     6	truncated unfold. Exit 0 iff EVERY requested file is complete.
     7	
     8	  fps_unfold_complete.py f1.root [f2 ...]   # explicit files
     9	  fps_unfold_complete.py --all              # the 10 canonical endpoints (relative to CWD=nd-unfolding)
    10	
    11	COMPLETE := TFile opens & not zombie; NOT TFile::kRecovered (recovery => truncated write);
    12	hXSecND_flat present with nbins==285 (FPS 15pt x 19pz extended grid), all bins finite, sum>0;
    13	globalCompleteness TParameter present, finite, > MIN_COMPLETE (the unfold's '[CHECK] c=' value).
    14	"""
    15	import argparse, os, sys
    16	import numpy as np
    17	import ROOT
    18	
    19	ROOT.gROOT.SetBatch(True)
    20	BANDS = ["BeamAngleX", "BeamAngleY", "MuonResolution",
    21	         "Muon_Energy_MINERvA", "Muon_Energy_MINOS"]
    22	OUTDIR = "active_universe_5d/fps/unfolds"
    23	NAME = "fps2d_xsec_MEFHC_5iter_lgbm_uni_full_{b}_{ep}.root"
    24	EXPECT_NBINS = 285          # 15 (pt) x 19 (pz) FPS extended grid
    25	MIN_COMPLETE = 0.50         # sanity floor; healthy unfolds report c=1.0000
    26	
    27	
    28	def check(path):
    29	    r = {"path": os.path.basename(path), "ok": False, "why": ""}
    30	    if not os.path.exists(path):
    31	        r["why"] = "missing"; return r
    32	    if os.path.getsize(path) < 1024:
    33	        r["why"] = f"tiny ({os.path.getsize(path)}B)"; return r
    34	    f = ROOT.TFile.Open(path)
    35	    if not f or f.IsZombie():
    36	        r["why"] = "zombie/unopenable"; return r
    37	    if f.TestBit(ROOT.TFile.kRecovered):
    38	        f.Close(); r["why"] = "kRecovered (truncated/uncleanly-closed write)"; return r
    39	    h = f.Get("hXSecND_flat")
    40	    if not h:
    41	        f.Close(); r["why"] = "no hXSecND_flat"; return r
    42	    nb = h.GetNbinsX()
    43	    if nb != EXPECT_NBINS:
    44	        f.Close(); r["why"] = f"nbins {nb} != {EXPECT_NBINS}"; return r
    45	    vals = np.array([h.GetBinContent(i + 1) for i in range(nb)])
    46	    if not np.all(np.isfinite(vals)):
    47	        f.Close(); r["why"] = "non-finite bins in hXSecND_flat"; return r
    48	    s = float(vals.sum())
    49	    if s <= 0:
    50	        f.Close(); r["why"] = f"sum<=0 ({s:.3e})"; return r
    51	    gc = f.Get("globalCompleteness")
    52	    gcv = float(gc.GetVal()) if gc else None
    53	    f.Close()
    54	    if gcv is None or not np.isfinite(gcv):
    55	        r["why"] = "no/NaN globalCompleteness"; return r
    56	    if gcv < MIN_COMPLETE:
    57	        r["why"] = f"globalCompleteness {gcv:.4f} < {MIN_COMPLETE}"; return r
    58	    r.update(ok=True, sum=s, gc=gcv, nbins=nb); return r
    59	
    60	
    61	def main():
    62	    ap = argparse.ArgumentParser()
    63	    ap.add_argument("files", nargs="*")
    64	    ap.add_argument("--all", action="store_true")
    65	    a = ap.parse_args()
    66	    files = list(a.files)
    67	    if a.all:
    68	        files = [os.path.join(OUTDIR, NAME.format(b=b, ep=ep)) for b in BANDS for ep in (0, 1)]
    69	    if not files:
    70	        sys.exit("no files given")
    71	    nbad = 0
    72	    for p in files:
    73	        r = check(p)
    74	        if r["ok"]:
    75	            print(f"[OK]   {r['path']} nbins={r['nbins']} sum={r['sum']:.4e} gc={r['gc']:.4f}")
    76	        else:
    77	            nbad += 1; print(f"[BAD]  {r['path']} :: {r['why']}")
    78	    print(f"\n{len(files) - nbad}/{len(files)} complete")
    79	    sys.exit(0 if nbad == 0 else 1)
    80	
    81	
    82	if __name__ == "__main__":
    83	    main()

    97	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    98	# Generic histogram + ROOT helpers (any dimensionality)
    99	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   100	def histnd(cols, w, edges):
   101	    """Weighted np.histogramdd over len(edges) axes -> (counts, sqrt(sumw2))."""
   102	    sample = np.column_stack(cols)
   103	    bins = [np.asarray(e, float) for e in edges]
   104	    counts, _ = np.histogramdd(sample, bins=bins, weights=w)
   105	    sumw2, _ = np.histogramdd(sample, bins=bins, weights=np.asarray(w) ** 2)
   106	    return counts, np.sqrt(sumw2)

   111	
   112	    The flat TH1D (bin = C-order ravel index over `arr.shape`) is what the
   113	    covariance/chi2 tools consume; the full N-D structure is recovered by
   114	    reshaping with the known per-axis edges. (A THnSparseD was tried but its
   115	    Python binding segfaults at 4D on this ROOT build, so we keep the robust flat
   116	    form; the binning lives in the edges, recorded in the docs and the 1D/2D
   117	    projections written alongside.)
   118	    """
   119	    ndim = arr.ndim
   120	    flat = arr.ravel(order="C")
   121	    flat_err = None if err is None else err.ravel(order="C")
   122	    h1 = ROOT.TH1D(name + "_flat", title + ";global bin;value", flat.size, 0, flat.size)
   123	    h1.SetDirectory(0)
   124	    for i, v in enumerate(flat, start=1):
   125	        h1.SetBinContent(i, float(v))
   126	        if flat_err is not None:
   127	            h1.SetBinError(i, float(flat_err[i - 1]))
   128	    f_out.cd(); h1.Write()
   129	
   130	    if ndim == 2:
   131	        h = u2d.make_th2d(name, title, edges[0], edges[1])
   132	        for ix in range(arr.shape[0]):
   133	            for iy in range(arr.shape[1]):
   134	                h.SetBinContent(ix + 1, iy + 1, float(arr[ix, iy]))
   135	                if err is not None:
   136	                    h.SetBinError(ix + 1, iy + 1, float(err[ix, iy]))
   137	        f_out.cd(); h.Write()
   138	
   139	
   140	def numpy_to_th1d(edges, vals, name, title):
   141	    h = u2d.make_th1d(name, title, edges)
   142	    for i, v in enumerate(vals, start=1):
   143	        h.SetBinContent(i, float(v))
   144	    return h

   148	# Generic multi-axis TTree readers (CV weights; pt/pz fixed + extra branches)
   149	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

   481	            n_zero += 1; continue
   482	        d = data_nd[coord]
   483	        if d <= 0.0:
   484	            n_zero += 1; continue
   485	        weights[i] = target[coord] / d
   486	    if verbose:
   487	        print(f"[INFO] measured training: sum={weights.sum():.6g} zero={n_zero}/{n}")
   488	    return weights

  1009	            ai = axis_names.index(args.closure_reweight_axis)
  1010	            _, y_unf = project_axis(xsec, edges, 2 + ai)
  1011	            _, y_ref = project_axis(ref_xsec, edges, 2 + ai)
  1012	            ratios = y_unf / np.where(y_ref > 0, y_ref, np.nan)
  1013	            print(f"  {args.closure_reweight_axis} 1D ratios: "
  1014	                  + ", ".join(f"{v:.3f}" for v in ratios))
  1015	            print(f"  injected mean factor={closure_rw_truthpass.mean():.4f}")
  1016	
  1017	    f_out.Close()
  1018	    print(f"[INFO] wrote {args.out}")
  1019	
  1020	
  1021	if __name__ == "__main__":
  1022	    main()

item.text: The main rollup does implement biased mean-centering, but its input gate is permissive: it groups whatever files match the regex and accepts any band with two or more entries. I’m now checking whether the current ten artifacts happen to satisfy the stronger contract, separately from whether the code enforces it.

     3	# --- root_6_28 activation (robust against default-module changes) ---
     4	# root_6_28 was built under conda 24.10.0. A 2026-07-02 change to the default `python`
     5	# module moved conda to a newer base (26.1.0) that no longer registers the env by NAME,
     6	# so the old `module load python; conda activate root_6_28` fails with
     7	# `EnvironmentNameNotFound`. We activate by FULL PREFIX via the creating base's hook, which
     8	# survives future default-module changes. Override the env path with ROOT628_PREFIX if it
     9	# ever moves. (sbatch/salloc set HOME to the real home, so $HOME resolves correctly here.)
    10	ROOT628_PREFIX="${ROOT628_PREFIX:-$HOME/.conda/envs/root_6_28}"
    11	ROOT628_CONDA="${ROOT628_CONDA:-/global/common/software/nersc/pe/conda/24.10.0/Miniforge3-24.7.1-0/bin/conda}"
    12	if [ -x "$ROOT628_CONDA" ] && [ -d "$ROOT628_PREFIX" ]; then
    13	    eval "$("$ROOT628_CONDA" shell.bash hook 2>/dev/null)"
    14	    conda activate "$ROOT628_PREFIX"
    15	else

/usr/bin/bash -lc "PYTHONDONTWRITEBYTECODE=1 /global/common/software/nersc/pe/conda/24.10.0/Miniforge3-24.7.1-0/bin/conda run --no-capture-output -p /global/homes/j/josephrb/.conda/envs/root_6_28 python - <<'PY'
import glob, os
import numpy as np
import ROOT
ROOT.gROOT.SetBatch(True)
base='nd-unfolding'
cvp=f'{base}/uq_fps/universe_sweep/fps2d_xsec_MEFHC_5iter_lgbm_uni_full_CV.root'
paths=sorted(glob.glob(f'{base}/active_universe_5d/fps/unfolds/fps2d_xsec_MEFHC_5iter_lgbm_uni_full_*_?.root'))

def flat(path,key='hXSecND_flat'):
    f=ROOT.TFile.Open(path); h=f.Get(key)
    a=np.array([h.GetBinContent(i+1) for i in range(h.GetNbinsX())],float)
    n=h.GetNbinsX(); f.Close(); return a,n

def edges2(path):
    f=ROOT.TFile.Open(path); h=f.Get('hXSecND')
    ex=np.array([h.GetXaxis().GetBinLowEdge(i+1) for i in range(h.GetNbinsX())]+[h.GetXaxis().GetBinUpEdge(h.GetNbinsX())])
    ey=np.array([h.GetYaxis().GetBinLowEdge(i+1) for i in range(h.GetNbinsY())]+[h.GetYaxis().GetBinUpEdge(h.GetNbinsY())])
    ndim=int(f.Get('ndim').GetVal()) if f.Get('ndim') else -1
    gc=float(f.Get('globalCompleteness').GetVal()) if f.Get('globalCompleteness') else float('nan')
    shape=(h.GetNbinsX(),h.GetNbinsY()); f.Close(); return ex,ey,shape,ndim,gc
cv,cvn=flat(cvp); cex,cey,cshape,cndim,cgc=edges2(cvp); rep=cv>0
print('CV',cvn,cshape,cndim,'rep',int(rep.sum()),'finite',bool(np.isfinite(cv).all()),'gc',cgc)
print('CV pt_edges',cex.tolist())
print('CV pz_edges',cey.tolist())
print('endpoint_count',len(paths))
for p in paths:
    a,n=flat(p); ex,ey,shape,ndim,gc=edges2(p)
    print(os.path.basename(p), 'n',n,'shape',shape,'ndim',ndim,'finite',bool(np.isfinite(a).all()),'sum',f'{a.sum():.17e}','gc',gc,
          'edges_eq_cv',bool(np.array_equal(ex,cex) and np.array_equal(ey,cey)),
          'zeros_on_cv_rep',int(np.sum(a[rep]==0)),'positive_mask_eq_cv',bool(np.array_equal(a>0,rep)))
PY"

/usr/bin/bash -lc "PYTHONDONTWRITEBYTECODE=1 /global/common/software/nersc/pe/conda/24.10.0/Miniforge3-24.7.1-0/bin/conda run --no-capture-output -p /global/homes/j/josephrb/.conda/envs/root_6_28 python - <<'PY'
import numpy as np
import ROOT
ROOT.gROOT.SetBatch(True)
B='nd-unfolding/uq_fps/corrected'
comb=f'{B}/universe_stage2_fps/uq_universe_fps_covariance_combined.root'
uth=f'{B}/unified_throw_cov_fps.root'
stat=f'{B}/uq_cov_stat_fps.root'; ml=f'{B}/uq_cov_mlsplit_fps.root'
pre=f'{B}/universe_stage2_fps/uq_universe_fps_covariance_combined_uthrow.root'
L={'BeamAngleX','BeamAngleY','MuonResolution','Muon_Energy_MINERvA','Muon_Energy_MINOS'}
V={'2p2h','CCQEPauliSupViaKF','FrAbs_pi','FrElas_N','HighQ2','LowQ2','MaCCQE','MaRES','MFP_N','MvRES','Rvn2pi','Rvp2pi','Flux'}

def th2(h):
 n=h.GetNbinsX(); m=h.GetNbinsY(); return np.array([[h.GetBinContent(i+1,j+1) for j in range(m)] for i in range(n)],float)
def load(path,key):
 f=ROOT.TFile.Open(path); h=f.Get(key)
 if not h: raise RuntimeError((path,key))
 a=th2(h); f.Close(); return a
def met(tag,a):
 s=.5*(a+a.T); ev=np.linalg.eigvalsh(s); scale=max(1e-300,np.max(np.abs(a))); mx=max(1e-300,abs(ev[-1]))
 print(tag,'shape',a.shape,'finite',bool(np.isfinite(a).all()),'rel_asym',f'{np.max(np.abs(a-a.T))/scale:.3e}',
       'min/maxeig',f'{ev[0]/mx:.3e}','sqrttr',f'{np.sqrt(max(0,np.trace(s))):.17e}')
fc=ROOT.TFile.Open(comb)
blocks={}
for k in fc.GetListOfKeys():
 name=k.GetName()
 if name.startswith('hCov_universe4d_') and name"'!='"'hCov_universe4d_total':
  blocks[name[len('hCov_universe4d_'):]]=th2(fc.Get(name))
Ccomb=th2(fc.Get('hCov_combined4d_total')); Ctot=th2(fc.Get('hCov_universe4d_total')); fc.Close()
Cstat=load(stat,'hCov_statfps_reported'); Cml=load(ml,'hCov_mlsplitfps_reported')
Call=sum(blocks.values(),np.zeros_like(Ccomb)); Clat=sum((blocks[x] for x in L),np.zeros_like(Ccomb)); Cvert=sum((blocks[x] for x in V),np.zeros_like(Ccomb))
Cnon=sum((v for k,v in blocks.items() if k not in L),np.zeros_like(Ccomb))

1	#!/usr/bin/env python3
     2	"""Small ROOT-free uncertainty helpers shared by production and tests."""
     3	
     4	from pathlib import Path
     5	
     6	import numpy as np
     7	
     8	
     9	def guarded_ratio(ratio, name="ratio", invalid_policy="error", clip=(1e-2, 1e2)):
    10	    """Validate a universe/CV ratio and make any neutralization explicit."""
    11	    ratio = np.asarray(ratio, dtype=float)
    12	    bad = ~np.isfinite(ratio) | (ratio <= 0.0)
    13	    if np.any(bad):
    14	        message = f"{name} contains {int(bad.sum())}/{ratio.size} non-finite or non-positive values"
    15	        if invalid_policy == "error":
    16	            raise ValueError(message)
    17	        if invalid_policy != "neutral":
    18	            raise ValueError(f"unknown invalid-ratio policy: {invalid_policy}")
    19	        print(f"[ratio][WARN] {message}; replacing with neutral ratio 1", flush=True)
    20	        ratio = ratio.copy()
    21	        ratio[bad] = 1.0
    22	    if clip is None:
    23	        return ratio
    24	    clipped = (ratio < clip[0]) | (ratio > clip[1])
    25	    if np.any(clipped):
    26	        print(f"[ratio][WARN] {name}: clipping {int(clipped.sum())}/{ratio.size} "
    27	              f"ratios to {clip}", flush=True)
    28	    return np.clip(ratio, *clip)
    29	
    30	
    31	def require_truth_ratio_bank(bank, bands, expected_flux=100):
    32	    """Require every truth-side +/- endpoint and an exact contiguous flux inventory."""
    33	    bank = Path(bank)
    34	    missing = [f"sig_{band}_t_{idx}.npy" for band in bands for idx in (0, 1)
    35	               if not (bank / f"sig_{band}_t_{idx}.npy").is_file()]
    36	    if missing:
    37	        raise ValueError(f"incomplete truth-ratio bank {bank}: missing {missing}")
    38	
    39	    flux_ids = set()
    40	    malformed = []
    41	    prefix = "sig_flux_t_"
    42	    for path in bank.glob(f"{prefix}*.npy"):
    43	        suffix = path.name[len(prefix):-4]
    44	        if suffix.isdigit():
    45	            flux_ids.add(int(suffix))
    46	        else:
    47	            malformed.append(path.name)
    48	    expected = set(range(int(expected_flux)))
    49	    if malformed or flux_ids != expected:
    50	        raise ValueError(
    51	            f"invalid flux bank {bank}: malformed={sorted(malformed)} "
    52	            f"missing={sorted(expected-flux_ids)} extra={sorted(flux_ids-expected)}"
    53	        )
    54	    return list(range(int(expected_flux)))
    55	
    56	
    57	def _positive_finite_ratio(ratio, name="ratio"):
    58	    ratio = np.asarray(ratio, dtype=float)
    59	    bad = ~np.isfinite(ratio) | (ratio <= 0.0)
    60	    if np.any(bad):
    61	        raise ValueError(
    62	            f"{name} contains {int(bad.sum())}/{ratio.size} non-finite or non-positive values"
    63	        )
    64	    return ratio
    65	
    66	
    67	def interpolate_asymmetric_ratio(g, rho_plus, rho_minus):
    68	    """Interpolate an asymmetric nuisance, preserving both +/-1 sigma branches.
    69	
    70	    rho(g) = rho_plus**g for g>=0 and rho_minus**(-g) for g<0.
    71	    Invalid or zero ratios are rejected instead of silently becoming CV.
    72	    """
    73	    plus = _positive_finite_ratio(rho_plus, "rho_plus")
    74	    minus = _positive_finite_ratio(rho_minus, "rho_minus")
    75	    if plus.shape != minus.shape:
    76	        raise ValueError(f"rho shape mismatch: {plus.shape} != {minus.shape}")
    77	    gg = np.asarray(g, dtype=float)
    78	    if not np.all(np.isfinite(gg)):
    79	        raise ValueError("nuisance g must be finite")
    80	    if gg.ndim == 0:
    81	        if float(gg) == 0.0:
    82	            return np.ones_like(plus)
    83	        return plus ** float(gg) if gg > 0 else minus ** float(-gg)
    84	    try:
    85	        gb = np.broadcast_to(gg, plus.shape)
    86	    except ValueError as exc:
    87	        raise ValueError(f"g shape {gg.shape} is not broadcastable to ratios {plus.shape}") from exc
    88	    out = np.ones_like(plus)
    89	    pos = gb > 0
    90	    neg = gb < 0
    91	    out[pos] = plus[pos] ** gb[pos]
    92	    out[neg] = minus[neg] ** (-gb[neg])
    93	    return out
    94	
    95	
    96	def mat_covariance(universes):
    97	    """MAT production convention: universe-mean centered, biased 1/N."""
    98	    X = np.asarray(universes, dtype=float)
    99	    if X.ndim != 2 or X.shape[0] < 2:
   100	        raise ValueError("universes must have shape (N>=2, nbins)")
   101	    if not np.all(np.isfinite(X)):
   102	        raise ValueError("universes contain non-finite values")
   103	    Z = X - X.mean(axis=0, keepdims=True)
   104	    return (Z.T @ Z) / X.shape[0]
   105	
   106	
   107	def joint_throw_covariance(throws, cv):
   108	    """Mean-centered joint covariance plus the separately reported mean shift."""
   109	    X = np.asarray(throws, dtype=float)
   110	    cv = np.asarray(cv, dtype=float)
   111	    if X.ndim != 2 or X.shape[1:] != cv.shape:
   112	        raise ValueError(f"throws/CV shape mismatch: {X.shape} vs {cv.shape}")
   113	    if X.shape[0] < 2 or not np.all(np.isfinite(X)) or not np.all(np.isfinite(cv)):
   114	        raise ValueError("need at least two finite throws and a finite CV")
   115	    mean = X.mean(axis=0)
   116	    return mat_covariance(X), mean - cv
   117	
   118	
   119	def project_covariance(covariance, projection):
   120	    C = np.asarray(covariance, dtype=float)
   121	    M = np.asarray(projection, dtype=float)
   122	    if C.ndim != 2 or C.shape[0] != C.shape[1]:
   123	        raise ValueError("covariance must be square")
   124	    if M.ndim != 2 or M.shape[1] != C.shape[0]:
   125	        raise ValueError(f"projection/covariance shape mismatch: {M.shape}, {C.shape}")
   126	    if not np.all(np.isfinite(C)) or not np.all(np.isfinite(M)):
   127	        raise ValueError("projection inputs must be finite")
   128	    return M @ C @ M.T
   129	
   130	
   131	def finite_observable_mask(coords, weights=None):
   132	    x = np.asarray(coords, dtype=float)
   133	    if x.ndim != 2:
   134	        raise ValueError("coords must have shape (events, observables)")
   135	    mask = np.all(np.isfinite(x), axis=1)
   136	    if weights is not None:
   137	        w = np.asarray(weights, dtype=float)
   138	        if w.shape != (x.shape[0],):
   139	            raise ValueError("weight/event shape mismatch")
   140	        mask &= np.isfinite(w)
   141	    return mask

   144	def active_selection_masks(truth, reco, pt_range, pz_range, reco_flags=None):
   145	    """ROOT-free selection kernel used to prove active-lateral migrations."""
   146	    truth = np.asarray(truth, dtype=float)
   147	    reco = np.asarray(reco, dtype=float)
   148	    if truth.shape != reco.shape or truth.ndim != 2 or truth.shape[1] < 2:
   149	        raise ValueError("truth and reco must have matching (events, >=2) shapes")
   150	    tfin = finite_observable_mask(truth)
   151	    rfin = finite_observable_mask(reco)
   152	    t = (tfin & (truth[:, 0] >= pt_range[0]) & (truth[:, 0] <= pt_range[1])
   153	         & (truth[:, 1] >= pz_range[0]) & (truth[:, 1] <= pz_range[1]))
   154	    r = (rfin & (reco[:, 0] >= pt_range[0]) & (reco[:, 0] <= pt_range[1])
   155	         & (reco[:, 1] >= pz_range[0]) & (reco[:, 1] <= pz_range[1]))
   156	    if reco_flags is not None:
   157	        flags = np.asarray(reco_flags, dtype=bool)
   158	        if flags.shape != (truth.shape[0],):
   159	            raise ValueError("reco flag/event shape mismatch")
   160	        r &= flags
   161	    return t, r

   135	    clipped = (rho < RHO_CLIP[0]) | (rho > RHO_CLIP[1])

   137	        print(f"[ratio][WARN] {label}: clipping {int(clipped.sum())}/{rho.size} "
   138	              f"ratios to {RHO_CLIP}", flush=True)
   139	    return np.clip(rho, *RHO_CLIP)

   142	def _knob_ratios(bank, bands, invalid_policy):
   143	    """Load both asymmetric endpoints for every knob and event-weight view."""
   144	    ratios = {}
   145	    for b in bands:
   146	        ratios[b] = {}
   147	        for view, stem in (("t", "sig_{b}_t_{idx}.npy"),
   148	                           ("r", "sig_{b}_r_{idx}.npy"),
   149	                           ("td", "td_{b}_{idx}.npy")):
   150	            minus = _ratio(_opt(bank, stem.format(b=b, idx=0)),
   151	                           f"{b}:{view}:-1", invalid_policy).astype(np.float32)
   152	            plus = _ratio(_opt(bank, stem.format(b=b, idx=1)),
   153	                          f"{b}:{view}:+1", invalid_policy).astype(np.float32)
   154	            ratios[b][view] = (plus, minus)
   155	    return ratios

   161	            _opt(bank, f"sig_flux_r_{u}.npy"),
   162	            _opt(bank, f"td_flux_{u}.npy"))

   165	def do_throws(args):
   166	    d, bands, n_flux = _load_bank(args.bank)
   167	    edges = d["edges"]
   168	    w_truth, w_reco, td_cv = d["w_truth"], d["w_reco"], d["td_w"]
   169	    ratios = _knob_ratios(args.bank, bands, args.invalid_ratio)
   170	
   171	    xs = []
   172	    metas = []
   173	    for j in range(args.throws):
   174	        gj = args.throw_offset + j
   175	        rng = np.random.default_rng(args.seed + gj)
   176	        g = {b: float(rng.standard_normal()) for b in bands}
   177	        rt = np.ones_like(w_truth); rr = np.ones_like(w_reco); rtd = np.ones_like(td_cv)
   178	        for b in bands:
   179	            rt *= interpolate_asymmetric_ratio(g[b], *ratios[b]["t"])
   180	            rr *= interpolate_asymmetric_ratio(g[b], *ratios[b]["r"])
   181	            rtd *= interpolate_asymmetric_ratio(g[b], *ratios[b]["td"])
   182	        wt_j = w_truth * rt; wr_j = w_reco * rr; wtd_j = td_cv * rtd
   183	        if n_flux:
   184	            u = int(rng.integers(n_flux))
   185	            fwt, fwr, fwtd = _flux_universe(args.bank, u)
   186	            wt_j *= _ratio(fwt, f"Flux:{u}:t", args.invalid_ratio)
   187	            wr_j *= _ratio(fwr, f"Flux:{u}:r", args.invalid_ratio)
   188	            wtd_j *= _ratio(fwtd, f"Flux:{u}:td", args.invalid_ratio)
   189	        else:
   190	            u = -1
   191	        # Systematic throws all use the SAME estimator seed. ML variation belongs
   192	        # exclusively in C_ML and must not leak into C_syst.
   193	        x = _xsec_for_weights(d, edges, wt_j, wr_j, wtd_j, args.iters, args.seed)
   194	        xs.append(x.ravel(order="C"))
   195	        metas.append((gj, u))
   196	        print(f"[throw {gj}] flux_u={u} sum(x)={x.sum():.4e}", flush=True)
   197	        # save incrementally so a killed job (e.g. interactive alloc expiry) keeps
   198	        # every completed throw rather than losing the whole slab.
   199	        _atomic_savez(args.out, xs=np.array(xs),
   200	                      throws=np.array([m[0] for m in metas]),

   202	                      seed=np.int64(args.seed),
   203	                      bands=np.array(bands, dtype=object))
   204	    print(f"[throws] wrote {args.out}: xs{np.array(xs).shape}")
